screens.name: Songs workspace
screens.purpose: Let the user scan the library and read a token-aligned digital lead sheet without changing the established workflow.
screens.primary_action: Open a chart from the library

ux_rules: Preserve the current songs workspace structure and reading flow while reducing repeated style literals.
Bind repeated warm palette, spacing rhythm, and typographic hierarchy decisions to the draft token baseline where practical.
Maintain strong selected-state contrast in the song list even if some values remain local for legibility.
Keep the lead-sheet surface editorial and warm without flattening functional distinctions between metadata, chart content, and annotations.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Keep the existing list-detail workspace intact while replacing repeated visual literals with token-backed values and roles.
interaction_risks: Selected-state contrast could weaken if token alignment over-normalizes the darker list treatment.
Lead-sheet notation may lose subtle hierarchy if all text roles are flattened into a small set of tokens without judgment.
notes: This run is about style normalization, not product behavior or data-shape changes.

constraints: Prefer CSS variable bindings from the draft token baseline over repeated hex values and repeated type literals.
Do not rewrite the songs layout architecture or introduce new shared abstraction layers unless they materially reduce duplication inside the scoped surface.
Preserve any local deviations that are still needed for selected-state contrast or notation readability, and record them if retained.
open_decisions: Whether the selected song card should eventually bind to a dedicated semantic inverse token set remains open.
Whether measure chips and directive badges should later use a more explicit semantic token layer remains open.

critical_judgments: The songs workspace should look materially the same in structure and tone after token alignment.
Repeated warm neutrals and typographic roles should map more clearly to shared token-backed values.
The selected state and chart readability should not regress.

visual_constraints: Keep the paper-like warm background and restrained stone palette.
Preserve display tracking and metadata rhythm that already match the selected profile.
Avoid introducing colder default theme colors from the base shadcn layer into the songs surface.
Retain a stronger inverse treatment for the selected card instead of over-softening all list states.
